_about: Verified Rust raid / cheapest-boom research dump. Source of truth for the ENTITIES + BOOM_METHODS constants in index.html. Numbers gathered June 2026; door damage values confirmed by the project owner against the current wipe.

_schema.methods[].sulfur: sulfur cost per single unit of that explosive
_schema.entities[].damage: REAL per-hit damage of each explosive to THIS entity (varies per material/face)
_schema.entities[].destroy: units to destroy alone = ceil(hp / damage); should match published raid charts
_schema.entities[].cheapest: absolute minimum sulfur to cross HP (single explosive or 2-explosive meta mix); often dozens of ammo shots
_schema.entities[].best: PRACTICAL pick: the CHEAPEST meta plan whose explosive-ammo shot count is <= ammoCap. Shots are fast (not penalised per-shot), just capped; over the cap, spend a rocket/C4 to shoot less. Plans may mix all three meta tools (e.g. C4 + rocket + ammo), not just two
_schema.ammoCap: max explosive-5.56 rounds tolerated in the 'best' plan; 50 (user: 40-50)
_schema.confidence: high = cross-confirmed; medium = derived or single-source; low = conflicting sources

_philosophy: Nobody raids with satchel+beancan (annoying to handle), and you empty 10 explosive rounds faster than you reload a rocket - so shots aren't penalised one-for-one, they're just capped. (1) Only meta tools (rocket, C4, ammo) are used for recommendations + mixes. (2) The app ranks the Pareto frontier from 'cheapest but most shots' to 'fewest shots'. (3) 'best' = the cheapest plan with ammo shots <= ammoCap (50). Examples: sheet door best = 1 rocket + 8 ammo (63 ammo is cheaper but >cap); garage stays 1 C4 + ~40 ammo (under cap); armored wall stays 7 C4 + 30 ammo (30 under cap); stone wall goes to 2 C4 (its 84-shot mix is over cap); sheet metal wall goes to the three-way mix 3 C4 + 1 rocket + 16 ammo (16 shots, 400 cheaper than pure 4 C4).
_uiDesign: Recommendation + full transparency, not auto-decide-everything. (a) Prominent: the rocket/C4+ammo plans ranked (the meta cross-product), with the capped 'best' highlighted. (b) Every row shows BOTH metrics - ammo shots AND total actions - purely informative, so the user can pick their own trade-off. (c) A dim single-explosive reference line lists all five tools incl. satchel/beancan (marked *), informative only, never picked as best. The cap drives the recommendation; the metrics and satchel/beancan are there to inform, not to decide.
_metaToolset: Only rocket, C4 and explosive 5.56 ammo (meta:true) are used for the recommended cheapest/best plans and their mixes. Satchel and beancan (meta:false) are unreliable/slow and shown as pure-cost reference only - never in the best mix. This matches how players actually raid.

methods.name: Rocket
methods.note: Raw sulfur from the craft tree (matches index.html RECIPE: 150 GP + 10 explosives). Some calculators show ~1450-1453 due to gunpowder batch rounding.

methods.name: C4 (Timed Explosive Charge)
methods.note: 20 explosives + 5 cloth + 2 tech trash; sulfur portion 2200.

methods.name: Explosive 5.56 Rifle Ammo
methods.note: Per round. Finest granularity, so usually the cheapest finisher and often the cheapest pure method on soft targets.

methods.name: Satchel Charge
methods.note: Reference only. Has a dud / re-light chance; effective cost higher.

methods.name: Beancan Grenade
methods.note: Reference only. Door charts imply 120 each; one stone-wall source implied 60 each (likely thrown-vs-stuck or data error).

methods.name: High Velocity Rocket
methods.note: Not used yet. ~40% less damage to buildings (~75 vs walls) but far cheaper; candidate to add to the meta set.

entities.name: Wooden Door
entities.note: Wood is weak; explosive ammo dominates. damage[ammo] tuned so ceil(200/12.6)=16. Realistically you'd just melee/fire it, but for explosives this is it.

entities.name: Sheet Metal Door
entities.note: KEY EXAMPLE. Cheapest is 63 ammo (1575) but nobody fires 63 shots. Practical best = 1 rocket (220 dmg, leaves 30 HP) + 8 ammo = 1600, only +25 sulfur for 9 actions instead of 63. Owner-confirmed. (If rocket does 236 not 220, the mix is even 1500 and also the absolute cheapest.)

entities.name: Garage Door
entities.note: Owner-confirmed: 3 rockets / 2 C4 / 150 ammo; best = 1 C4 + ~40 ammo. C4 does 440 -> 1 C4 leaves 160 HP -> 40 ammo (owner cited 41; ±1 round is within tolerance). Here cheapest IS practical: dropping the ~40 shots would need 2 C4 (+37%), not worth it.

entities.name: Armored Door
entities.note: Owner-confirmed: 1000 HP / 3 C4 / 250 ammo; optimum = 2 C4 + 30 ammo. C4 corrected 400 -> 440 (2 C4 = 880, leaves 120 -> 30 ammo). 3 C4 would be 6600, so the mix wins and is also practical here.

entities.name: Stone Wall
entities.note: Rocket 137 to hard side (cross-confirmed). C4 275 to all walls. Cheapest = 83 ammo + 1 C4 (4275; the C4 covers the big remainder, so 1 fewer ammo than '1 C4 + 84 ammo' = 4300). 83 shots is over the 50 cap, so practical best = 2 C4 (4400, +125 to skip the shots). High External Stone Wall is identical (500 HP).

entities.name: Sheet Metal Wall
entities.note: CONFLICT: rocket count 7 (rusthelp) vs 8 (rocket 137 -> ceil(1000/137)=8); using 8. C4 275 -> 4. Cheapest 3 C4 + 70 ammo (8350) is over the 50 cap. Practical best is the THREE-WAY mix 3 C4 + 1 rocket + 16 ammo (8400): 825+137 dmg leaves 38 HP -> 16 ammo, only 16 shots and 400 cheaper than pure 4 C4 (8800). Best example of why the solver mixes all three meta tools, not just two.

entities.name: Armored Wall
entities.note: 2000 HP (NOT 1000 - older charts had 1000). C4 275 -> 8. Best = cheapest = 7 C4 + 30 ammo (16150); 30 shots is under the 50 cap, so no reason to pay +1450 for pure 8 C4 (17600). ammo damage 2.504 so ceil(2000/2.504)=799.

additionalEntities_unverified.name: High External Stone Wall
additionalEntities_unverified.note: Same as Stone Wall (rocket 4 / C4 2 / ammo ~185/173).

additionalEntities_unverified.name: High External Wood Wall
additionalEntities_unverified.note: Not researched in detail; weak to fire/ammo.

additionalEntities_unverified.name: Ladder Hatch / Floor / Ceiling
additionalEntities_unverified.note: Same tier-resistances as the matching wall material; not added.

caveats: Damage varies per entity AND per face (soft inside vs hard outside). Wall values here are hard side. Doors have no soft/hard split.
Lumpy explosives (rocket, C4) have entity-specific damage; granular ones (satchel/beancan/ammo) are tuned so ceil(hp/damage) matches published destroy-counts.
Rocket vs sheet metal door: 220 (gamerempire, owner-confirmed) vs 236 (another source). Using 220; either way the practical best is 1 rocket + a few ammo.
Sheet metal wall: 7 rockets (rusthelp) vs 8 (rocket=137 dmg). Using 8.
Armored door: OWNER-CONFIRMED 1000 HP / 3 C4 / 250 ammo, optimum 2 C4 + 30 ammo (C4 damage = 440).
Garage door: OWNER-CONFIRMED 600 HP / 3 rockets / 2 C4 / 150 ammo, best 1 C4 + ~40 ammo (owner cited 41; ±1 round ignored).
Beancan sulfur: 120 (door charts) vs 60 (one stone-wall figure). Using 120.
Rocket sulfur modelled as 1400 (raw craft, matches the app); some calculators show ~1450 from batch rounding.
Recommendations + mixes use ONLY meta tools (rocket, C4, explosive ammo). Satchel/beancan are reference-only (meta:false); HV/incendiary rocket not included yet.

The app shows the Pareto frontier of plans (non-dominated on sulfur vs ammo shots), not just single explosives - this is the 'Kreuzprodukt' of mixes. Cheapest = top of frontier (most shots); each step down spends a bit more to shoot less.

PRACTICAL 'best' vs absolute 'cheapest': best = the cheapest plan with explosive-ammo shots <= ammoCap (50). Shots are NOT penalised one-for-one (you fire faster than you reload), they're just capped; over the cap the solver adds a rocket/C4 to drop below it. Satchel/beancan never qualify (annoying to handle).
Solver assumes you can finish a weakened entity with the finisher (true in-game).

sources.name: rusthelp.com
sources.use: Entity HP + destroy counts + sulfur totals (current)

sources.name: gamerempire.net
sources.use: Per-hit rocket damage to doors (220) and C4 to garage (440)

sources.name: tradeit.gg
sources.use: Explosives chart, sulfur per explosive, C4 275/rocket 137 walls

_meta.gameUpdateRef: 904.83-era recipes; raid charts last verified ~2026-06-01 per sources
